perturbation.original_text: All documents and other tangible objects containing or representing Confidential Information and all copies thereof which are in the possession of Receiving Party shall be and remain the property of the Disclosing Party and shall be promptly returned to the Disclosing Party upon the Disclosing Party’s request.
perturbation.changed_text: All documents and other tangible objects containing or representing Confidential Information and all copies thereof which are in the possession of Receiving Party shall be and remain the property of the Disclosing Party and shall be promptly returned to the Disclosing Party upon the Disclosing Party’s request. However, the Receiving Party may retain one archival copy of the Confidential Information for legal compliance purposes.
perturbation.explanation: This modification creates an in-text contradiction in section 7. The original text states that all documents and tangible objects must be returned. The changed text adds that the Receiving Party can retain one archival copy for legal compliance purposes. While seemingly benign, this creates an exception that contradicts the 'all documents' clause. The phrase is being contradicted because the section defines how information is supposed to be handled, but contradicts itself on how to handle the return of all documents.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All documents and other tangible objects containing or representing Confidential Information and all copies thereof which are in the possession of Receiving Party shall be and remain the property of the Disclosing Party and shall be promptly returned to the Disclosing Party upon the Disclosing Party’s request. However, the Receiving Party may retain one archival copy of the Confidential Information for legal compliance purposes. Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant any rights to either party under any patent, mask work right or copyright of Company, nor shall this Agreement grant Receiving Party any rights in or to Confidential Information except as expressly set forth herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the original requirement that 'all documents and other tangible objects' be returned to the Disclosing Party. The addition of 'However, the Receiving Party may retain one archival copy of the Confidential Information for legal compliance purposes' directly contradicts the initial 'all' clause, creating a clear and direct conflict. This falls under the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type, as different sections of the contract now provide conflicting obligations regarding the return of documents, making enforcement unclear.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall survive for a period of 3 years from the date of disclosure of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall survive for a period of 3 years from the date of disclosure of the Confidential Information. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the obligations concerning trade secrets shall survive indefinitely.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds a contradiction to Section 9. Initially, the agreement lasts 3 years. The added clause states that obligations concerning trade secrets last indefinitely. This is contradictory because some information defined as confidential information may also qualify as a trade secret. If this is the case the confidentiality obligations for trade secrets last forever and the rest only for 3 years. Which leads to in-text contradiction regarding which terms should be enforced, leading to disputes over contractual obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall survive for a period of 3 years from the date of disclosure of the Confidential Information. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the obligations concerning trade secrets shall survive indefinitely. The Receiving Party agrees that any violation or threatened violation of this Agreement will cause irreparable injury to the Dis- closing Party, entitling the Disclosing Party to obtain injunctive relief in addition to all legal remedies.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause stating obligations concerning trade secrets last indefinitely, while the original text states the entire agreement lasts 3 years. Since confidential information may include trade secrets, this creates an in-text contradiction as to whether the confidentiality obligations for trade secrets last forever or only for 3 years. The contradicted text which is the same text as the changed text, highlights this contradiction because it contains both conflicting statements within the same paragraph, making enforcement unclear, in accordance to the definition of inconsistencies - in text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Any and all disputes arising under or related to this Agreement shall be adjudicated exclusively in the Commonwealth of Massachusetts.
perturbation.changed_text: Any and all disputes arising under or related to this Agreement shall be adjudicated exclusively in the Commonwealth of Massachusetts. However, disputes concerning intellectual property rights may be adjudicated in any jurisdiction where infringement is alleged to have occurred.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction in the final clause of Section 11 by allowing intellectual property disputes to be adjudicated outside of Massachusetts. This directly contradicts the earlier statement that *all* disputes will be adjudicated exclusively in Massachusetts. This creates uncertainty as parties may now dispute where to file legal claims concerning intellectual property, leading to uncertainty and dispute.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This docu-ment contains the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. Any failure to enforce any provision of this Agreement shall not constitute a waiver thereof or of any other provision hereof. This Agreement may not be amended, nor any obligation waived, except by a writing signed by both parties hereto. Any and all disputes arising under or related to this Agreement shall be adjudicated exclusively in the Commonwealth of Massachusetts. However, disputes concerning intellectual property rights may be adjudicated in any jurisdiction where infringement is alleged to have occurred. The parties have executed this Nondisclosure Agreement as of the date first above written.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that *any and all* disputes arising under or related to the agreement would be adjudicated exclusively in Massachusetts. The changed text introduces an exception for intellectual property disputes, allowing them to be adjudicated in any jurisdiction where infringement is alleged. This directly contradicts the initial statement regarding *all* disputes and the contradicted text, creating an in-text contradiction as different sections of the contract provide conflicting obligations, falling under the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees that it shall take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure and unau- thorized use of the Confidential Information. Without limiting the foregoing, Receiving Party shall take at least those measures that Receiving Party takes to protect its own most highly confidential information and shall have its employees, if any, who have access to Confidential Information sign a nonuse and non-disclosure agreement in content substantially similar to the provi-sions hereof, prior to any disclosure of Confidential Information to such employees.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party agrees that it shall take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure and unau- thorized use of the Confidential Information. Without limiting the foregoing, Receiving Party shall take at least those measures that Receiving Party takes to protect its own confidential information, except in cases of legal subpoena. The Receiving Party shall have its employees, if any, who have access to Confidential Information sign a nonuse and non-disclosure agreement in content substantially similar to the provi-sions hereof, prior to any disclosure of Confidential Information to such employees.
perturbation.explanation: The changed text in Section 4 creates an in-text contradiction. The original paragraph states the Receiving Party must take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy. The added text says that exception to this is cases of legal subpoena. However, if the Receiving Party must take all reasonable measures and it is not limited in that obligation, but there is a subpoena, then the original obligation and the added text creates a contradiction. The obligation is being contradicted due to the legal subpoena, making the extent of the original obligation potentially void.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party agrees that it shall take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure and unau- thorized use of the Confidential Information. Without limiting the foregoing, Receiving Party shall take at least those measures that Receiving Party takes to protect its own confidential information, except in cases of legal subpoena. The Receiving Party shall have its employees, if any, who have access to Confidential Information sign a nonuse and non-disclosure agreement in content substantially similar to the provi-sions hereof, prior to any disclosure of Confidential Information to such employees. The Receiving Party shall not make any cop - ies of Confidential Information unless the same are previously approved in writing by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception (legal subpoena) to the Receiving Party's obligation to take all reasonable measures to protect confidential information. This directly contradicts the original, unqualified obligation to protect the secrecy of the Confidential Information, leading to uncertainty about the extent of that obligation. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, highlighting that the contradiction is within the same clause due to the modification. The addition of 'except in cases of legal subpoena' creates a loophole where the 'all reasonable measures' obligation can be bypassed, thus making the text contradictory.
